volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: The Women of Trachis (Section 346 - Section 480)

sections.label: Section 346
sections.original: ἁνὴρ ὅδʼ οὐδὲν ὧν ἔλεξεν ἀρτίως
sections.translation: That man Lichas spoke nothing of what he just told you in strict accordance with the truth . He was just now lying , or else he was dishonest in his earlier report .

sections.label: Section 349
sections.original: τί φής ; σαφῶς μοι φράζε πᾶν ὅσον νοεῖς .
sections.translation: What do you say ? Explain to me clearly all that you mean ,

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: ἃ μὲν γὰρ ἐξείρηκας ἀγνοία μʼ ἔχει .
sections.translation: for I cannot understand what you said .

sections.label: Section 351
sections.original: τούτου λέγοντος τἀνδρὸς εἰσήκουσʼ ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: I heard this man declare , before many witnesses , that it was for the sake of this girl that Heracles overthrew Eurytus and the high towers of Oechalia : Eros ,

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: μόνος θεῶν θέλξειεν αἰχμάσαι τάδε ,
sections.translation: alone of the gods , enchanted him into doing those deeds of arms , not the toilsome servitude to Omphale in Lydia , nor the death to which Iphitus was hurled . But now the herald has thrust Eros aside and tells a different tale . Well , when Heracles could not persuade him whose seed produced the child

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: τὴν παῖδα δοῦναι , κρύφιον ὡς ἔχοι λέχος ,
sections.translation: to give him the girl for his secret concubine , he devised some petty complaint as a pretext , and made war upon her fatherland , in which , as the herald said , that Eurytus ruled . He killed the king , her father , and

sections.label: Section 365
sections.original: ἔπερσε . καὶ νῦν , ὡς ὁρᾷς , ἥκει δόμους
sections.translation: sacked her city . And now , as you see , he makes his return , sending her to this house not without consideration , lady , and not as if she were to be a slave . No , do not expect that ; it is not likely , if his heart has been kindled with desire . On this account , my Queen , I resolved to reveal to you all

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: δέσποινʼ , ὃ τοῦδε τυγχάνω μαθὼν πάρα .
sections.translation: that I had heard from that man . Many others were listening to it , as I was , in the public place where the Trachinians were assembled , so they can convict him . If my words are unwelcome , I am grieved ; but nevertheless I have spoken the truth .

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα , ποῦ ποτʼ εἰμὶ πράγματος ;
sections.translation: Ah , misery ! What is happening to me ? What secret plague have I welcomed beneath my roof ? Ah , me ! So , she was born without a name , as her escort swore ?

sections.label: Section 379
sections.original: ἦ κάρτα λαμπρὰ καὶ κατʼ ὄνομα καὶ φύσιν ,
sections.translation: No , illustrious by name , as by birth ;

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: πατρὸς μὲν οὖσα γένεσιν Εὐρύτου ποτὲ
sections.translation: she is the daughter of Eurytus , and was once called Iole . But he could say nothing of her birth , because , of course , he made no investigation .

sections.label: Section 383
sections.original: ὄλοιντο — μή τι πάντες οἱ κακοί , τὰ δὲ
sections.translation: May destruction seize not all wrongdoers — no , only the man who practices stealthy wrongdoing that is shameful to his rank !

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: τί χρὴ ποεῖν , γυναῖκες ; ὡς ἐγὼ λόγοις
sections.translation: Ah , friends , what should I do ? I am dumbstruck by this latest news !

sections.label: Section 387
sections.original: πεύθου μολοῦσα τἀνδρός , ὡς τάχʼ ἂν σαφῆ
sections.translation: Go and question Lichas , since he might tell the truth , if you were willing to compel him by threat of force to answer .

sections.label: Section 389
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἶμι · καὶ γὰρ οὐκ ἀπὸ γνώμης λέγεις .
sections.translation: I will go . Your advice does not lack good judgment .

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: ἡμεῖς δὲ προσμένωμεν ; ἢ τί χρὴ ποεῖν ;
sections.translation: And I , shall I wait here ? Or what do you require ?

sections.label: Section 391
sections.original: μίμνʼ , ὡς ὅδʼ ἀνὴρ οὐκ ἐμῶν ὑπʼ ἀγγέλων ,
sections.translation: Stay here — since he is coming out from the house without my summons , but of his own accord .

sections.label: Section 393
sections.original: τί χρή , γύναι , μολόντα μʼ Ἡρακλεῖ λέγειν ;
sections.translation: My lady , what message shall I bear to Heracles ? Instruct me , for , as you see , I am going .

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: ὡς ἐκ ταχείας σὺν χρόνῳ βραδεῖ μολὼν
sections.translation: How quickly you rush away , when your arrival had been so tardy — before we have even resumed our talk .

sections.label: Section 397
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἴ τι χρῄζεις ἱστορεῖν , πάρειμʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Well , if there is anything you must ask , I am at your service .

sections.label: Section 398
sections.original: ἦ καὶ τὸ πιστὸν τῆς ἀληθείας νέμεις ;
sections.translation: Will you indeed give me the honest truth ?

sections.label: Section 399
sections.original: ἴστω μέγας Ζεύς , ὧν γʼ ἂν ἐξειδὼς κυρῶ .
sections.translation: Yes , be great Zeus my witness — in anything that I know .

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: τίς ἡ γυνὴ δῆτʼ ἐστὶν ἣν ἥκεις ἄγων ;
sections.translation: Who is the woman , then , whom you escorted here ?

sections.label: Section 401
sections.original: Εὐβοιίς · ὧν δʼ ἔβλαστεν οὐκ ἔχω λέγειν .
sections.translation: She is Euboean . But whose offspring , I cannot say .

sections.label: Section 402
sections.original: οὗτος , βλέφʼ ὧδε · πρὸς τίνʼ ἐννέπειν δοκεῖς ;
sections.translation: You there , look at me . To whom do you think you speak ?

sections.label: Section 403
sections.original: σὺ δʼ εἰς τί δή με τοῦτʼ ἐρωτήσας ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: And you , to what end have you asked me such a question ?

sections.label: Section 404
sections.original: τόλμησον εἰπεῖν , εἰ φρονεῖς , ὅ σʼ ἱστορῶ .
sections.translation: Dare to answer my question , if you are sensible .

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: πρὸς τὴν κρατοῦσαν Δῃάνειραν , Οἰνέως
sections.translation: I speak to the royal Deianeira , daughter of Oeneus , wife of Heracles and , unless my eyes deceive me , my mistress .

sections.label: Section 408
sections.original: τοῦτʼ αὔτʼ ἔχρῃζον , τοῦτό σου μαθεῖν · λέγεις δέσποιναν εἶναι τήνδε σήν ;
sections.translation: The very word that I wished to hear from you — you admit that she is your mistress ?

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: τί δῆτα ; ποίαν ἀξιοῖς δοῦναι δίκην ,
sections.translation: Well , then , what penalty would you think it right to pay , if you should be found to be behaving wrongly towards her ?

sections.label: Section 412
sections.original: πῶς μὴ δίκαιος ; τί ποτε ποικίλας ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Wrongly ? What fiction have you created ?

sections.label: Section 413
sections.original: οὐδέν · σὺ μέντοι κάρτα τοῦτο δρῶν κυρεῖς .
sections.translation: None . Rather it is you , to be sure , who do this .

sections.label: Section 414
sections.original: ἄπειμι · μῶρος δʼ ἦ πάλαι κλύων σέθεν .
sections.translation: I am leaving — I was foolish to listen to you so long .

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: οὔ , πρίν γʼ ἂν εἴπῃς ἱστορούμενος βραχύ .
sections.translation: No , not until you have answered a small question .

sections.label: Section 416
sections.original: λέγʼ , εἴ τι χρῄζεις · καὶ γὰρ οὐ σιγηλὸς εἶ .
sections.translation: Speak , if you require something . You are not the silent type .

sections.label: Section 417
sections.original: τὴν αἰχμάλωτον , ἣν ἔπεμψας ἐς δόμους , κάτοισθα δήπου ;
sections.translation: That captive , whom you escorted into the house , you know whom I mean ?

sections.label: Section 418
sections.original: φημί · πρὸς τί δʼ ἱστορεῖς ;
sections.translation: Yes . But why do you ask ?

sections.label: Section 419
sections.original: οὔκουν σὺ ταύτην , ἣν ὑπʼ ἀγνοίας ὁρᾷς ,
sections.translation: Did you not avow that she , on whom you look as if in ignorance ,

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: Ἰόλην ἔφασκες Εὐρύτου σπορὰν ἄγειν ;
sections.translation: was Iole , the seed of Eurytus , your captive ?

sections.label: Section 421
sections.original: ποίοις ἐν ἀνθρώποισι ; τίς πόθεν μολὼν
sections.translation: To whom on earth did I say it ? Who and where is the man who heard me say it and will be your witness ?

sections.label: Section 423
sections.original: πολλοῖσιν ἀστῶν · ἐν μέσῃ Τραχινίων
sections.translation: To many of our own citizens you said it . In the public gathering of Trachinians a great crowd heard this much , at least , from you .

sections.label: Section 424
sections.original: ἀγορᾷ πολύς σου ταῦτά γʼ εἰσήκουσʼ ὄχλος .
sections.translation: Sure ,

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: κλύειν γʼ ἔφασκον · ταὐτὸ δʼ οὐχὶ γίγνεται
sections.translation: they said they heard . But it does not amount to the same thing to tell one ’ s fancy and to give an accurate report .

sections.label: Section 427
sections.original: ποίαν δόκησιν ; οὐκ ἐπώμοτος λέγων
sections.translation: What do you mean , fancy ! Did you not speak on your oath when you said that you were bringing her as a bride for Heracles ?

sections.label: Section 429
sections.original: ἐγὼ δάμαρτα ; πρὸς θεῶν , φράσον , φίλη
sections.translation: I ? Bringing a bride ? In the name of the gods , dear

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: δέσποινα , τόνδε τίς ποτʼ ἐστὶν ὁ ξένος .
sections.translation: mistress , tell me who on earth this stranger is ?

sections.label: Section 431
sections.original: ὃς σοῦ παρὼν ἤκουσεν , ὡς ταύτης πόθῳ
sections.translation: I am a man who heard from your own lips that the conquest of the whole city was due to desire for this girl and that the Lydian woman did not destroy it , but the passion which arose for the girl .

sections.label: Section 434
sections.original: ἅνθρωπος , ὦ δέσποινʼ , ἀποστήτω · τὸ γὰρ
sections.translation: Lady , send this creature away .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: νοσοῦντι ληρεῖν ἀνδρὸς οὐχὶ σώφρονος .
sections.translation: To babble with the insane is not the mark of a sensible man .

sections.label: Section 436
sections.original: μή , πρός σε τοῦ κατʼ ἄκρον Οἰταῖον νάπος
sections.translation: No , I implore you by Zeus whose strikes lightning down along the high glens of Oeta , do not rob me of a true report ! Your words will not fall on the ears of a cruel woman , nor has she yet to learn the fate of humanity ,

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: χαίρειν πέφυκεν οὐχὶ τοῖς αὐτοῖς ἀεί .
sections.translation: how by our nature we are often inconstant in our pleasures . He does not use good sense , then , who like a boxer opposes the blows of Eros . For Eros rules the gods as he pleases , and he rules me as well — that is certain . Why not another woman , just like me ?

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: ὥστʼ εἴ τι τὠμῷ τʼ ἀνδρὶ τῇδε τῇ νόσῳ
sections.translation: And so I am crazed indeed , if I blame my husband because that disease has seized him or because he was seized by this woman , his partner in a thing which is no shame to them , and no wrong to me . Impossible ! If it was from him that you learned

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: ψεύδει , μάθησιν οὐ καλὴν ἐκμανθάνεις .
sections.translation: to lie , it was not a noble lesson . But if you are your own teacher in this , you will be seen as cruel when it is your wish to prove kind . No , tell me the whole truth , since to a free-born man the name of liar attaches like an ugly stain .

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: ὅπως δὲ λήσεις , οὐδὲ τοῦτο γίγνεται ·
sections.translation: If your hope is to escape detection , that cannot come to pass : many are those whom you told and who will inform me . And if you are afraid , your fear is mistaken , since it is not learning the truth that would pain me . But to know it , what is terrible in that ? Has

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: πλείστας ἀνὴρ εἷς Ἡρακλῆς ἔγημε δή ;
sections.translation: Heracles not all by himself already mated with a multitude of other women ? And never yet has one of them come away with a harsh word or reproach from me , nor shall this girl , even if she should melt in her passion . Indeed , I felt a profound pity when I saw her because

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: τὸ κάλλος αὐτῆς τὸν βίον διώλεσεν ,
sections.translation:
her beauty has destroyed her life , and she , unfortunate one , has against her will devastated her fatherland and enslaved it .
Well , those things must go with the wind . But as for you , I tell you , be dishonest to others , but always speak the truth to me .

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: πείθου λεγούσῃ χρηστά , κοὐ μέμψει χρόνῳ
sections.translation: Obey her good and kind advice , and hereafter you will neither have cause to complain of this lady , nor lack my thanks .

sections.label: Section 472
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ φίλη δέσποινʼ , ἐπεί σε μανθάνω
sections.translation: Indeed , then , dear mistress . Since I see that you think as mortals should think and not without good judgment , I will tell you the whole truth , and not hide it .

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: ἔστιν γὰρ οὕτως ὥσπερ οὗτος ἐννέπει .
sections.translation: Yes , it is just as this one says . That terrible longing for the girl long ago shot through Heracles , and for her sake the desolate Oechalia , her father ’ s land , was leveled by his spear . But he — I must say what is in his favor —

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: οὔτʼ εἶπε κρύπτειν οὔτʼ ἀπηρνήθη ποτέ ,
sections.translation:
he never ordered me to conceal the fact and never denied it . Instead I , lady , fearing to wound your heart by such news , erred — if you regard this in any way an error .
Since , however , you now know the whole story ,
